reasoning: The question requires the 30-day protest count to exceed the 360-day average of ~3.167. Because protest events are discrete variables, this requires at least 4 protests to occur in the 30-day window. Protest occurrences generally follow a heavily right-skewed, overdispersed distribution (the median is lower than the mean). For a process with a mean of 3.167, the probability of drawing a value of 4 or higher is roughly 33% to 39% (using standard Negative Binomial or Poisson assumptions). Furthermore, the 360-day average in this case stretches back to late 2024, likely capturing the tail end of the intense 'vie chère' cost of living protests that spiked on the island, thereby inflating the 12-month average above typical localized baselines in late 2025. Given the right-skewness of the distribution and the lack of specific evidence of a major sustained resurgence in late October 2025, I assign baseline estimates below 50%. As per the instructions, uncertainty is increased (moved closer to 0.5) for the more distant resolution dates.
